name: Microsoft Agent Framework
description: Open-source SDK and runtime for building AI agents and graph-based multi-agent workflows in .NET and Python. Merges AutoGen and Semantic Kernel into a single framework with checkpointing, middleware, and OpenTelemetry-based observability.

evidence.source: Microsoft Foundry Blog - Agent Framework Introduction
evidence.value: Combines AutoGen's multi-agent orchestration research with Semantic Kernel's production-grade plumbing

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Assessment of agent execution quality based on framework capabilities, AutoGen/Semantic Kernel lineage, and community-reported results since public preview

evidence.value: Typed function tools, MCP support, and middleware pipeline for intercepting and validating tool calls in .NET and Python

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Review of tool abstraction design, type safety, MCP integration, and middleware-based tool call validation

evidence.value: Graph-based workflows support sequential, concurrent, handoff, and group-chat orchestration patterns with explicit control flow

multi_step_planning.methodology: Evaluation of graph-based workflow engine for complex task decomposition and deterministic orchestration

evidence.value: Built-in thread state management, pluggable memory providers, and workflow checkpointing for pause/resume of long-running tasks

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of thread persistence, checkpointing, and memory provider abstractions

evidence.source: Microsoft Foundry Blog - Agent Framework Introduction
evidence.value: Checkpointing enables resuming failed workflows from last saved state; middleware allows custom retry and error-handling policies

error_recovery.methodology: Assessment of checkpoint/resume semantics and middleware-based error handling under failure injection scenarios

evidence.value: Inherits AutoGen's multi-agent patterns: group chat, handoffs, nested agents-as-tools, and agent-to-agent (A2A) protocol support

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent orchestration pattern review covering group chat, handoff, and concurrent workflows

evidence.value: No built-in execution sandbox; tool isolation is the developer's responsibility, with optional hosted code interpreter via Azure AI Foundry

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of tool execution boundaries in self-hosted and Azure-hosted configurations

evidence.value: Integrates with Microsoft Entra ID and Azure RBAC when used with Azure AI Foundry; middleware enables custom authorization on tool calls

access_control.methodology: Review of identity integration, RBAC support, and middleware-based authorization hooks

evidence.source: Microsoft Foundry Blog - Agent Framework Introduction
evidence.value: Middleware pipeline supports content filtering and guardrail integration (e.g., Azure AI Content Safety), but no default injection defense is enabled

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Assessment of available guardrail integration points versus out-of-the-box injection protections

evidence.value: Self-hosted runtime keeps agent state in developer-controlled stores; threads and workflow state are isolated per execution

data_isolation.methodology: Data architecture review of thread isolation and state storage control

evidence.value: MIT licensed, fully open source for both .NET and Python, with public roadmap and active issue tracker; 1.0 GA released 2026-04-03
Self-hosted SDK stores no data itself; retention is fully controlled by the developer's chosen state and memory stores

data_retention.methodology: Privacy architecture review of framework data handling

evidence.value: GDPR-compliant deployments achievable; Azure-hosted components covered by Microsoft's GDPR commitments, self-hosted compliance depends on configuration

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capability assessment for self-hosted and Azure-backed deployments

evidence.value: Data flows only to the configured model provider (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, local models); framework itself sends no telemetry by default

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of model provider connectors and telemetry defaults

evidence.value: Runs anywhere .NET or Python runs; supports local models via Ollama, Foundry Local, and ONNX connectors

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment including local model support

evidence.value: Comprehensive Microsoft Learn documentation, tutorials, and official migration guides from AutoGen and Semantic Kernel

documentation_quality.methodology: Documentation completeness review including migration guides

evidence.source: Microsoft Foundry Blog - Agent Framework Introduction
evidence.value: Native OpenTelemetry instrumentation following GenAI semantic conventions; traces agent runs, tool calls, and workflow steps

execution_traceability.methodology: Observability review of built-in OTel spans for agent and workflow execution

evidence.value: Explicit graph-based workflows make control flow inspectable; agent reasoning visibility depends on underlying model and logging configuration

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of workflow visualizability and reasoning trace availability

evidence.value: MIT license, monorepo for .NET and Python SDKs, public development with community contributions accepted

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of license and development model

evidence.value: Rapid star growth since October 2025 preview; consolidated AutoGen and Semantic Kernel communities migrating to the framework as both enter maintenance mode

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis of GitHub activity, discussions, and migration momentum

evidence.value: Simple ChatClientAgent abstraction; pip/NuGet install; connectors for Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, and local models

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment for .NET and Python developers

evidence.source: Microsoft Foundry Blog - Agent Framework Introduction
evidence.value: Designed for production with stateless agent design, durable checkpointed workflows, and hosting options from containers to Azure AI Foundry

scalability.methodology: Scalability assessment of runtime design and hosting options

evidence.value: Free MIT-licensed framework; costs arise only from chosen model provider and hosting infrastructure
First-class OpenTelemetry support exports traces and metrics to any OTel backend including Azure Monitor, Jaeger, and Aspire dashboard

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Monitoring features assessment of built-in telemetry

evidence.value: 1.0 GA released 2026-04-03 with stable API surface; designated successor to Semantic Kernel and AutoGen, which are now in maintenance mode

production_readiness.methodology: Production readiness assessment of GA status, API stability, and Microsoft support commitment

code-generation.notes: Strong foundation for building coding agents with typed tools and checkpointed workflows, though not a turnkey coding agent itself

customer-support.notes: Handoff and group-chat orchestration patterns plus Azure ecosystem integration suit enterprise support agent systems

data-analysis.notes: Graph workflows with checkpointing handle long-running analysis pipelines; code interpreter available via Azure AI Foundry

research-assistant.notes: Multi-agent orchestration inherited from AutoGen works well for researcher/critic/synthesizer patterns

content-creation.notes: Capable multi-agent content pipelines, though less purpose-built for creative workflows than role-based frameworks

financial-analysis.notes: Enterprise identity, observability, and self-hosting make regulated deployments feasible with custom compliance work

best_for: Enterprises standardizing on a single Microsoft-backed agent framework across .NET and Python
Teams migrating from AutoGen or Semantic Kernel, which are now in maintenance mode
Production systems needing durable, checkpointed, observable multi-agent workflows
Organizations already invested in the Azure AI Foundry ecosystem
strengths: Unifies AutoGen's multi-agent research patterns with Semantic Kernel's production engineering
Graph-based workflows with checkpointing for durable, resumable long-running tasks
Native OpenTelemetry instrumentation for tracing agents, tools, and workflows
First-class support for both .NET and Python with consistent abstractions
MIT-licensed and fully open source with strong Microsoft backing and 1.0 GA stability
Middleware pipeline enables custom guardrails, auth, and policy enforcement on every tool call
limitations: No built-in execution sandbox; tool isolation must be implemented by the developer
Young framework (GA April 2026); ecosystem of extensions still smaller than older frameworks
Deepest integrations favor the Azure ecosystem, which may not suit cloud-neutral teams
Migration from AutoGen and Semantic Kernel requires code changes despite official guides
Prompt injection defenses require explicit guardrail integration rather than safe defaults

supported_models: Local models via Ollama and Foundry Local
metadata.pricing: Free (MIT license) - Costs only from model provider and hosting
metadata.predecessors: Official successor to AutoGen and Semantic Kernel (both maintenance mode)
